supersedes: The v1 baseline (recorded 2026-07-09) measured the DELETED object/JCS serving carrier. This v2 baseline re-establishes the anchor on the RFC 9421 + RFC 9530 serving path (ADR-MCPRE-050 sole carrier), same box, same 128/8000/1-core anchor config. RFC 9421 header-signature + Content-Digest is CHEAPER than JCS canonicalization: throughput +27.7% (4591.9 -> 5866.4 rps), p50/p99/p999 added latency all ~20% lower. The old numbers are not comparable to the current wire and are retired.
caveat: Recorded on a single developer workstation with the load generator CO-LOCATED with the proxy on the same cores. At the concurrency this anchor uses the run is client-concurrency-bound (128 concurrent cold mTLS handshakes; client-side Ed25519/TLS crypto costs as much as server-side), so throughput is a stable REGRESSION anchor but is NOT a production capacity number, and the 1->N core sweep below is recorded-but-NON-AUTHORITATIVE: on one box the loadgen contends with the fleet for cores and flattens the curve. Authoritative absolute SLOs and the real scaling curve require a dedicated load-generator host driving the proxy on the production hardware class (MCPRE-110 production half; the GKE fleet run, now at the canonical 128/8000 envelope).

recorded.carrier: RFC 9421 HTTP Message Signatures + RFC 9530 Content-Digest (signature in HTTP headers; no object/JCS _meta signature)

anchor.comment: The regression anchor: the least loadgen-distorted, most reproducible point (single served core, moderate concurrency). Values are the MEDIAN of 3 reps; throughput held within +/-1% across reps. `adr051_slo_gate.py` compares a fresh run of THIS config against these numbers under the local_regression tolerances in adr-051-slo-targets.json.

recorded_core_sweep_non_authoritative.comment: Recorded for the record only. Throughput is FLAT-to-noisy as served cores rise because the co-located loadgen is the limiter, NOT because the per-core fleet fails to scale (the 4-core dip is loadgen contention noise, not a serving regression). Do NOT gate on this curve. RFC 9421 carrier, cold mode, concurrency 128, 8000 requests each.
